description: Education sector standards bundle. Covers FERPA (student records), COPPA (under-13 parental consent), NIST SP 800-171 (CUI for grant-funded research), ISO 27001 baseline, GDPR (EU operations including child consent), and WCAG 2.2 AA (K-12 and higher ed accessibility). Shipped in BranchNuX v0.3.

standards.name: FERPA — Right to Inspect Educational Records
standards.description: Parents and eligible students have the right to inspect and review the student's education records maintained by the school. Schools must comply with a request for access to records within 45 days. Schools are not required to provide copies of records unless, for reasons such as great distance, it is impossible for parents or eligible students to review the records.

standards.name: FERPA — Consent Required for Disclosure
standards.description: Schools must have written permission from the parent or eligible student before releasing any information from a student's education record. Schools may disclose records without consent to school officials with legitimate educational interest, to other schools where the student seeks enrollment, to federal/state authorities for audit/evaluation, in connection with financial aid, to accrediting organizations, to parents of a dependent student, in an emergency, and in response to judicial orders.

standards.name: FERPA — Annual Notification of Rights
standards.description: Schools must annually notify parents and eligible students of their FERPA rights. The notification must include the right to inspect and review education records, the right to seek to amend education records, the right to consent to disclosure of personally identifiable information, the right to file a complaint with the U.S. Department of Education, and the procedure for exercising these rights.

standards.name: FERPA — Directory Information Policy
standards.description: Schools may disclose directory information (student name, address, phone, email, dates of attendance, degrees awarded, etc.) without consent if they give annual public notice of directory information categories and provide parents and eligible students the right to opt out within a reasonable time. Schools that choose to disclose directory information must notify students of the categories and the right to refuse.

standards.name: COPPA — Verifiable Parental Consent for Under-13 Users
standards.description: Operators of websites or online services directed to children under 13, or operators with actual knowledge that they are collecting personal information from children under 13, must obtain verifiable parental consent before collecting, using, or disclosing personal information from children. Consent methods must be reasonably calculated to ensure that the person providing consent is the child's parent.

standards.name: COPPA — Direct Notice to Parents
standards.description: Prior to collecting personal information from a child, operators must provide clear and prominent notice to parents that: describes the types of personal information collected, how the information is used, and whether it is disclosed to third parties; gives parents the option to consent to collection and use of information without consenting to disclosure to third parties; provides parents with a means to review and delete child's personal information; and provides contact information for the operator.

standards.name: COPPA — Prohibition on Conditioning Participation on Collection
standards.description: An operator is prohibited from conditioning a child's participation in an activity on the child disclosing more personal information than is reasonably necessary to participate in the activity. Educational platforms must permit use of core features without requiring collection of personal information beyond what is educationally necessary.

standards.name: NIST 800-171 — Limit System Access to Authorized Users
standards.description: Limit system access to authorized users, processes acting on behalf of authorized users, and devices (including other systems). Institutions receiving federal grants or contracts involving Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) must implement access control aligned to NIST SP 800-171 as a condition of the award. DFARS clause 252.204-7012 requires institutions to implement these controls.

standards.name: NIST 800-171 — Create and Retain System Audit Logs
standards.description: Create and retain system audit logs and records to the extent needed to enable the monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity. Audit logs must be retained for a period aligned to the research data retention requirements of the funding agency.

standards.name: NIST 800-171 — Encryption in Transit for CUI
standards.description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI during transmission unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards. Use FIPS 140-2/3 validated encryption modules. TLS 1.2 minimum for all transmission of research data, student data, or federal grant data across public networks.
standards.family: NIST SP 800-171 — 3.13: System and Communications Protection

standards.name: ISO 27001 — Access Control Policy
standards.description: Rules to control physical and logical access to information and other associated assets shall be established and implemented based on business and information security requirements. Access control policy must address need-to-know, need-to-use principles, and separation of duties. Privileged access must be formally authorized and regularly reviewed.
standards.family: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 — Annex A: Information Security Controls

standards.name: ISO 27001 — Use of Cryptography
standards.description: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including cryptographic key management, shall be defined and implemented. Policy must specify approved algorithms, key lengths, key management procedures, and circumstances requiring encryption. Education institutions holding research data or personal data must apply cryptographic controls to data at rest and in transit.
standards.family: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 — Annex A: Information Security Controls

standards.name: GDPR — Lawful Basis for Processing
standards.description: Processing of personal data of EU data subjects (including EU students and staff) shall be lawful only if at least one of the following applies: the data subject has given consent; processing is necessary for performance of a contract; processing is necessary for compliance with a legal obligation; processing is necessary to protect vital interests; processing is necessary for a public task; processing is necessary for legitimate interests. Educational institutions must document their lawful basis for each processing activity.

standards.name: GDPR — Child Consent for Online Services
standards.description: Where consent is the lawful basis for processing and it relates to the direct offer of information society services to a child (under 16 in most EU member states; under 13 in some), processing shall be lawful only if the holder of parental responsibility has given or authorized consent. Institutions must make reasonable efforts to verify that consent was given or authorized by the holder of parental responsibility, taking into account available technology.

standards.name: GDPR — Transparency — Privacy Notice at Point of Collection
standards.description: Where personal data are collected directly from the data subject, the controller shall at the time of collection provide: identity and contact details of the controller, purposes and legal basis of processing, legitimate interests pursued (if applicable), recipients or categories of recipients, intended transfers to third countries, retention period, rights of the data subject (access, rectification, erasure, portability, objection), whether provision of data is statutory/contractual, and the existence of automated decision-making.

standards.name: WCAG 2.2 AA — Contrast (Minimum)
standards.description: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1, except for large text (18pt or 14pt bold) which requires at least 3:1. This is critical for educational platforms used by students with low vision. Verify contrast ratios for all body text, labels, error messages, and UI text using automated tooling (Axe, WAVE) and manual spot-checks.

standards.name: WCAG 2.2 A — Non-text Content
standards.description: All non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose. Educational images, diagrams, charts, graphs, and infographics must have descriptive alt text or extended descriptions. Decorative images must have empty alt attributes (alt='').

standards.name: WCAG 2.2 AA — Status Messages
standards.description: In content implemented using markup languages, status messages can be programmatically determined through role or properties such that they can be presented to the user by assistive technologies without receiving focus. Educational LMS platforms must expose quiz score feedback, submission confirmations, and progress updates via ARIA live regions (role='status' or role='alert') so screen reader users receive them without losing focus.
